findings.finding: Working-tree window between T5 (canonical wrapper deletion) and T13 step 1 (uv reinstall) breaks shell-level cortex-update-item invocations; in-session tooling that does backlog write-backs would fail in this window. Now flagged in T13 Context as Working-tree caution.
findings.reviewer_angle: Single-commit atomicity vs working-tree intermediate state
findings.evidence_quote: After this commit lands and users run uv tool install -e . --reinstall, the command names resolve to entry-point binaries instead of the deleted wrappers.

findings.finding: No drift-detection mechanism between bin/cortex-log-invocation (bash) and cortex_command/backlog/_telemetry.py (Python); spec Non-Requirement #7 forbids unification while R12 demands byte-equivalence. Mitigation applied: Task 12 now includes a byte-equivalence pytest comparing actual JSONL bytes from both implementations.
findings.reviewer_angle: Telemetry byte-equivalence claim contradicts spec Non-Requirement #7
findings.evidence_quote: Byte-equivalence is asserted but the two implementations remain physically separate.

findings.finding: Breadcrumb-category contract was lossier than the bash shim (5 categories collapsed to 2 in original T9 prose). Mitigation applied: T9 now enumerates all 5 categories (no_session_id, no_repo_root, session_dir_missing, write_denied, other).
findings.reviewer_angle: Telemetry byte-equivalence claim contradicts spec Non-Requirement #7
findings.evidence_quote: resolve LIFECYCLE_SESSION_ID from env; if absent or session dir missing, write a breadcrumb to ~/.cache/cortex/log-invocation-errors.log and return silently

findings.finding: After T8 set-union, a future maintainer adding both a bin/cortex-X script and a [project.scripts] cortex-X entry would not trigger any diagnostic (set-union deduplicates). Surfaced in Veto Surface as a deferred coverage hole; the user may want to add an E004 source-ambiguity diagnostic in a future ticket.
findings.reviewer_angle: Parity linter deployed semantics on fresh-clone / CI
findings.evidence_quote: W005 (allowlist-superfluous) stays scoped to bin/-discovered commands only; entry-point names are out of scope for the allowlist mechanism in this ticket.

findings.finding: Spec R11 codified the new entry-point-orphan W003 case as verified manually before commit, then test entry removed; left no permanent regression coverage. Mitigation applied: T8 now requires extending SELF_TEST_CASES with a synthetic pyproject.toml entry-point-orphan fixture.
findings.reviewer_angle: Parity linter deployed semantics on fresh-clone / CI
findings.evidence_quote: an artificial test where pyproject.toml declares an unwired entry point (e.g., cortex-orphan-test = "cortex_command.test:main") and no doc references it triggers W003 (verified manually before commit, then test entry removed).

findings.finding: Original T7 audit grep covered only python3 -m cortex_command.<sub> module-form invocations; missed shell-form cortex upgrade invocations (currently absent in tree but a future maintainer-friendliness gap). Mitigation applied: T7 now includes a second sweep for shell-form cortex upgrade invocations.
findings.reviewer_angle: Carve-out removal regression — dashboard initiator and runner-child paths
findings.evidence_quote: Audit command exactly as-spec d: grep -rn "python3 -m cortex_command." --include="*.md" --include="*.sh" --include="*.toml" --include="Justfile" --include="justfile" .
